title: Rebuild Canon
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild: Canon???             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Nov 08, 2007 11:01 pm With Evangelion as "a story that repeats itself" Can we rely on it as cannon? Say if rebuild "seems" to answer some older questions about the original not that it will Can we can we actually say that the question was answered correctly?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 08, 2007 11:03 pm So far, it seems to be its own continuity. But we'll see, it may end up being the same as the TV series.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Nov 09, 2007 1:20 am If it is indeed true that Rebuild proves that time is a circle, then it can be considered a sequel. Although, since it is like a reboot, I consider it a canon unto itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Fri Nov 09, 2007 1:28 am I think the real question is "Is Rebuild actually necessary?"

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Fri Nov 09, 2007 1:31 am It's all an elaborate plan to get the new generation snared. Anno needs the revenue, so he's getting new teens to like the series, and since Rebuild isn't gonna be just a retelling, they'll all go back and watch the old series, but since it isn't airing, they have to buy it. So they spend money. Then they buy EOE. Then they buy merchandise. Rinse, lather, repeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Nov 09, 2007 1:32 am Well, we won't know until we know the final outcome. Besides, if they are making such a commitment as to make four new movies, then I think they deemed it necessary. If not, just make a PSP called Rebuild of Evangelion taking the player through the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Fri Nov 09, 2007 1:36 am Shiro wrote: It's all an elaborate plan to get the new generation snared. Anno needs the revenue, so he's getting new teens to like the series, and since Rebuild isn't gonna be just a retelling, they'll all go back and watch the old series, but since it isn't airing, they have to buy it. So they spend money. Then they buy EOE. Then they buy merchandise. Rinse, lather, repeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Nov 09, 2007 2:18 am It's not Anno, it's mainly Gainax. Anno's already expressed disdaine at tthe incredible amount of Eva merchandise out there, so I seriously doubt he'd wholly support the system.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Fri Nov 09, 2007 2:27 am Indigo Arcangel wrote: It's not Anno, it's mainly Gainax. Anno's already expressed disdaine at tthe incredible amount of Eva merchandise out there, so I seriously doubt he'd wholly support the system.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Nov 09, 2007 2:29 am Indigo Arcangel wrote: It's not Anno, it's mainly Gainax. Anno's already expressed disdaine at tthe incredible amount of Eva merchandise out there, so I seriously doubt he'd wholly support the system.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Fri Nov 09, 2007 10:57 am I really hope Gainax doesn't tie Rebuild with the old series. They said they wanted it to be more accessible to people who've never seen. Having the films tie into the old series would brake that, plus it would make people have to watch the old series to know whats going on.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Fri Nov 09, 2007 2:14 pm No, I don't think it can be considered canon for the TV series. It's 'canon' all by itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Nov 09, 2007 6:00 pm I think it would be quite possible to make this into a sequal withought it being inaccessible to new audiences. You just have to keep things ambiguous in the same sort of Eva-ish way that we're used to. People would therefore become interested in the older series, but they wouldn't have to watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Nov 09, 2007 6:09 pm In a completely unrelated question way off topic- I don't have time today to rant and rave as per usual (sidenote- I believe anything Eva based by Anno himself immediately becomes cannon to some degree or another) My simple question is one that I don't think I've ever heard come up - Does Hikari in any way shape or form show up in Eva 1.0. I know her part would be limited to one or two lines at best but that question hit me today at work. And for such a popular 2nd tier character I've not heard a thing. Later

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 09, 2007 9:45 pm From what I can make out, Hikari Horaki is nowhere in sight.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 09, 2007 9:49 pm Wow. That's an interesting observation, I didn't notice that in the Rebuild 1.0 audio! :shock: Somewhat ironic, considering Newtype mentioned 1.0 will be a "school setting" and one of the two Theatrical Posters feature Hikari along with the other students on there... Anyway, it's really too early to tell whether or not Rebuild is Canon with the original series/movie. If it is, I think it's possible that it'll be done right (Eva IS Anno's baby, after all ;)), but I guess only time will tell either way.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 09, 2007 9:57 pm Newtype also put Asuka in those posters, IIRC, and Asuka isn't introduced in 1.0. I listened to the part where Toji and Kensuke leave to see the Shamshel fight, because they have a conversation with Hikari at that point. If they had to put the class rep in, that's the place to do it. And Toji and Kensuke are clearly seen in the official trailers so far, and even Pen-pen is seen, but no Hikari.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Fri Nov 09, 2007 10:59 pm so if no hikari in1.0 can we expect to see her in the future?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Nov 09, 2007 11:07 pm EvaCub wrote: so if no hikari in1.0 can we expect to see her in the future?

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Nov 09, 2007 11:48 pm I fail to see how a movie that's essentially a remake of the original series can be considered canon.
